mnemonics.title: Diagonalization Steps
mnemonics.content: P.E.D: Pick eigenvalues, Find eigenvectors, Diagonalize (P⁻¹AP = D)

mnemonics.title: Diagonalizable Conditions
mnemonics.content: A.S.S: Algebraic multiplicity Sum = n and Same for each eigenvalue (geometric = algebraic)

mnemonics.title: Symmetric Matrix Properties
mnemonics.content: S.E.R.F: Symmetric → Eigenvalues are Real, can make matrix Orthogonal for diagonalization, Real eigenvectors, Form orthonormal basis

acronyms.expansion: P: Matrix of eigenvectors (invertible), D: Diagonal eigenvalue matrix, P⁻¹: Inverse of eigenvector matrix
acronyms.usage: Standard form for diagonalization A = PDP⁻¹

acronyms.expansion: Q: Orthogonal matrix (Q⁻¹=Qᵀ), A: Original symmetric matrix, D: Resulting diagonal matrix

visualTips.visualization: Imagine stretching or compressing a rubber sheet along certain directions - those directions are eigenvectors, the stretch factor is eigenvalue
Think of P as camera angle change, D as viewing the matrix in 'simple form', P⁻¹ as rotating back to original view
A symmetric matrix is like a stretched sphere - rotating to principal axes shows only lengths along each axis (eigenvalues)

analogies.analogy: Like converting coordinates from a slanted/skewed grid to a standard rectangular grid - makes operations simpler
Same person photographed from different angles - underlying identity (linear transformation) same, representation differs
Video game: eigenvector is the direction of a joystick movement, eigenvalue is how much the game scales that movement
